questions.question: Why keep crawl/index/rank boundaries explicit in a search engine?
options: To isolate failures and tune each stage independently
To remove ranking from the design
questions.explanation: Clear interfaces let you test tokenization without crawling, test indexing without ranking, and test ranking with a known index. When relevance drops, you can identify which stage introduced the problem.

questions.question: What is an inverted index and why is it called 'inverted'?
options: A mapping from terms to documents, inverting the document-to-terms relationship
A sorted array of all words
questions.explanation: A forward index maps documents to their terms. An inverted index inverts this: it maps each term to the list of documents containing it. This makes query-time term lookup O(1) instead of scanning all documents.

questions.question: What does an inverted index optimize?
options: Term-to-document lookup efficiency for query processing
questions.explanation: Without an inverted index, a query for 'algorithms' would scan every document. With an inverted index, you directly look up the posting list for 'algorithms' and get the matching documents in O(1). Multi-term queries intersect posting lists.

questions.question: How does BM25 differ from basic TF-IDF?
options: BM25 adds document length normalization and term frequency saturation
BM25 only works for short queries
questions.explanation: BM25 normalizes for document length (long documents naturally have more term occurrences) and saturates term frequency (the 100th occurrence of a term matters less than the 2nd). These adjustments produce more robust rankings than raw TF-IDF.

questions.question: Why begin with a simple ranking baseline like TF-IDF?
options: To establish measurable behavior before adding complex relevance features
questions.explanation: A baseline ranking lets you measure improvement. If BM25 gives better results than TF-IDF on your test queries, you know the improvement is from the ranking function, not from indexing bugs. Without a baseline, you can't measure progress.

_meta.title: Build a Search Engine (Crawler + Index + Ranker)
